agent_raw:
The Smart Shortlist pilot retrospective does not support a general-availability launch decision. Four interacting confounds undermine the 34% lift claim.

First, adoption was self-selected. Employers activated Smart Shortlist during an active search through normal customer-success intake rather than a central experiment queue. Employers who opt into a new product during an active search are likely more engaged with the platform and more sophisticated about recruiting than the platform average. The 34% lift compares activated employers to the platform average — not to a comparable group of non-activated employers.

Second, the reporting window (February through May) overlaps exactly with the graduation-linked recruiting season noted in the context paragraph. The marketplace identifies that entry-level demand and candidate responsiveness concentrate around graduation cycles. If the reporting window captures the seasonal peak in engagement, the interview-to-application rate improvement would be elevated for any employer group, not specifically for Smart Shortlist users.

Third, the ATS integration advantage conflates model quality with employer sophistication. The 41% lift for ATS-integrated employers versus 22% for dashboard-only employers is presented as evidence that richer context helps the ranking model. But ATS-integrated accounts are also large enterprise customers with more established recruiting operations. The integration tier and employer maturity are confounded.

Fourth, regional enablement was staggered (West Coast February, Midwest March, Southeast April) and results are reported at an aggregate level. West Coast employers get a longer measurement window and may also be in different hiring cycles than Southeast employers in April. The aggregate 34% conceals this heterogeneity.
all_issues_raised: Employer self-selection: opt-in activation selects engaged employers vs platform average
Graduate recruiting season: February-May window overlaps with graduation-cycle peak
Integration selection: ATS-connected employers are also large enterprise accounts
Regional enablement timing: staggered start dates with aggregate reporting

empirical_test.condition: Randomized encouragement or staged enablement test, or matched difference-in-differences analysis within same recruiting periods, regions, and integration tiers
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: After balancing employer mix, recruiting season, region, and integration tier, interview-to-application improvement falls to near zero or becomes inconsistent
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Within matched employers in same recruiting windows and activation tiers, randomly enabled accounts show durable lift of at least 10%
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Feature helps a subset of integrated enterprise employers but most of the aggregate gap is explained by adopter mix, season, and region timing
